title: Bumelia neglecta
intro: Bumelia neglecta, also known as the wild plum or American dog plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bumelia neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Bumelia neglecta is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and is also used in tinctures and infusions to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Bumelia neglecta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is also known as the "Neglected Bumelia" or "Bumelia." More specifically, Bumelia neglecta is classified within the genus Bumelia, which consists of about 15 species of trees and shrubs. The plant's classification is part of its broader taxonomic context within the plant kingdom.
